name: Netlify API
what_it_does: Netlify's REST API for managing site deployments, builds, functions, forms, identity, and DNS configuration on the Netlify hosting platform.
use_cases: Triggering site builds and deployments programmatically
Managing Netlify Functions and environment variables
Creating and managing site configurations via agents
Monitoring build status and deployment health
Managing DNS records and custom domains
not_for: Applications requiring persistent servers or stateful backends
Teams deploying non-static or non-serverless workloads at scale
Teams requiring on-premise or self-hosted deployment
best_when: An agent needs to manage Netlify-hosted sites including deployments, build configuration, and environment variables.
avoid_when: You need persistent server processes or are not using Netlify for hosting.

auth.notes: Personal access tokens are the simplest auth method for agents. OAuth2 available for multi-user integrations. Tokens grant full account access — no fine-grained scopes.

pricing.notes: Free tier is generous for small projects. Build minute limits can be hit quickly by agents triggering frequent builds.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Standard REST error responses. Build failure logs available through separate logs endpoint. Error messages are adequate but not as detailed as Vercel.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: No idempotency keys. Repeated deploy triggers create multiple build jobs.
known_agent_gotchas: Personal access tokens have full account access — no scope limitation is a security concern
Build minute limits on free tier can be exhausted by agents triggering frequent builds
OpenAPI spec available but may have gaps vs actual API
Deploy previews and production deploys have different URL patterns requiring careful handling
Environment variable updates require a new deploy to take effect
